scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Strong rule extraction (12 rules with individual confidence) and excellent gap analysis (8 clarifying questions, 'demand' clearly flagged as undefined). However, two hard fail criteria are triggered: (1) confidence.level is 72, well above the pass threshold of 40 and above the fail threshold of 65; (2) Given/When/Then testable assertions are written for every single rule despite the rubric requiring none be written from such vague input. Saved from full fail by the exceptional quality of the Critical Gaps section and the volume of clarifying questions, which demonstrate genuine domain skepticism even as the confidence score contradicts it.

observations.text: Extracted 12 rules with individual confidence ratings — exceeds the 5-rule minimum substantially
8 critical gaps surfaced in a dedicated table — exceeds the 5-question minimum
'Demand' is explicitly called out as undefined in both the ubiquitous language section and the gaps table
Guardrail vagueness, competitive benchmarking, and profitability scope all flagged as insufficient
Quote validity/price lock, emergency override, and rate-of-change limits are sophisticated domain questions
confidence.level of 72 is nearly double the pass ceiling of 40 and exceeds the fail threshold of 65 — fundamentally miscalibrated for input this vague
Given/When/Then testable assertions written for all 12 rules despite rubric explicitly requiring none from vague input
Most rules rated HIGH confidence when the input is a vague CEO quote — Rules 1-5, 7-8, 12 all marked HIGH despite massive undefined terms

The bottom-line summary acknowledges '8 critical gaps must be resolved before any team writes a line of code' yet the body treats the rules as well-defined enough for testable assertions — internal contradiction
Specific invented parameters appear in assertions (e.g., 'price >= cost + minimum_margin', 'price_floor >= cost_of_delivery') without flagging these as invented
